skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Creates detailed, test-focused implementation plans before code changes begin.
skill.summary: Turn requirements into bite-sized implementation plans with files, tests, commands, and handoff steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are false positives from Markdown examples and inline command text, not executable code. Static weak-cryptography findings are text matches with no cryptographic implementation. The only confirmed concern is intended filesystem output to docs/plans, which is low risk and appropriate for a planning skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and sample pytest or git commands. No evidence found that SKILL.md executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are inside Markdown formatting and instructional examples. There is no script file, shell wrapper, or executable code path in the scanned skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations contain natural language or plan template text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of the cited lines shows ordinary prose and template placeholders. There are no crypto APIs, hashes, encryption routines, or security-sensitive comparisons.

low_findings.title: Plan Output Writes to Workspace Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs generated plans to be saved under docs/plans. This is expected behavior, but users should review generated file paths before allowing writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The save path is explicit and limited to documentation output. The risk is low because there is no destructive write instruction or secret access pattern.

content.value_statement: Complex requirements often fail when developers start coding without a shared plan. This skill turns specs into focused tasks with files, tests, commands, and handoff steps.
actual_capabilities: Creates implementation plans from specs or requirements before coding starts.
Breaks work into small tasks with clear file-level guidance.
Adds test-first steps with expected pass and fail outcomes.
Includes exact commands and expected results for each task.
Defines an execution handoff after the plan is saved.
limitations: Does not inspect code automatically unless the assistant reads project files.
Does not execute the generated plan by itself.
May need user review for project-specific commands and paths.
Depends on accurate requirements and current repository context.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Convert a feature brief into tasks with files, tests, and commit checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Prepare Work for Another Developer
use_cases.description: Create a complete handoff that explains architecture, test design, and task order.

use_cases.title: Structure Test-First Changes
use_cases.description: Write a plan that starts each implementation step with a failing test and verification command.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Plan from Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short feature brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to create an implementation plan for this requirement: [paste requirement]. Include tasks, files, tests, and commands.

prompt_templates.title: Plan with Repository Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request after relevant files are known.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill. Review these relevant files first: [list files]. Then create a plan that names exact files to create, modify, and test.

prompt_templates.title: TDD Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a risky refactor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to plan this refactor: [describe refactor]. Make every task test-first, include expected failures, and keep commits small.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Team Handoff Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for work another session or developer will execute.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to produce a handoff-ready implementation plan for [project goal]. Include architecture, task order, verification, and execution options.

output_examples.input: Add account export settings to an existing web application.
output_examples.output: A plan with a goal, architecture notes, task sections, exact files to modify, test-first steps, verification commands, and commit checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Refactor billing validation without changing behavior.
output_examples.output: A staged refactor plan that starts with characterization tests, updates one component at a time, and lists expected test results.

output_examples.input: Prepare a new developer to implement search filters.
output_examples.output: A handoff document that explains context, paths, implementation sequence, test coverage, and execution choices.

best_practices: Provide the current requirements, relevant files, and any known constraints before planning.
Review generated paths and commands before saving or executing the plan.
Keep each task small enough to test and commit independently.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for reading the repository.
Do not accept vague steps that omit files, tests, or expected outcomes.
Do not execute generated commands without checking they match the project.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It writes implementation plans that describe code changes, tests, commands, and handoff steps.

faq.question: Can it save the plan to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill asks the assistant to save plans under docs/plans using a dated feature filename.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide requirements, relevant files, project constraints, and any testing expectations.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No. It may include command examples in the plan, but it does not execute them automatically.

faq.question: When should I avoid using it?
faq.answer: Avoid it for trivial one-line fixes where a detailed plan would slow the work.
